Hi, this is Mark Mehling. Right now you are feeling deep grief for
the one you love. It is not the best time to be making quick
decisions about real estate. Although selling the bouse may be your
immediate thought, tbe housing market in Florida is way down, the
house is full of memories as well as personal items, and
you could save thousands of dollars with just a
little time to evaluate your best options. While
you catch your breath, separating the pain
from financial decisions, you can bave the
home professionally managed for a small fee.
You gain time to think, deal with probate or
other legalities, and handle the house decision
from a little distance. The house, meanwhile,
gets the professional attention it needs to
survive Florida weather, proper upkeep to
increase its sale-ability, and you have someone
you can call at anytime. If you decide to sell, I
can make recommendations about real estate
agents, moving companies, estate sales, and
donations. You can be involved as much- or as
little- as you like. The choices are yours, tbe
work is mine.
Mark Mehling, author
of the popular book
"Leaving Your Home-
ALONE"
Call me personally, I can help- in total
confidence, if needed. Finally, you can relax
knowing that one part of the difficult decisions
of life are a whole lot easier.
HASTY HOUSE DECISIONS COST BIG $$$$
After saying goodbye to your loved one, the next question is always
"What will we do with the house?" You need time to think because
quick decisions now will cost you thousands later. The house is full
of memories and memorabilia. Sell it? The market is way down riqht
now. And what about the contents? Maybe keep it for a family
vacation home- you may never have the chance to own a Florida
home again. You need time to consider all the alternatives. And
the house needs professional care while it sits empty to keep it's
value- and valuable contents- free from mold and other risks not
covered by homeowner's insurance.
Put Time on Your Side, without house
worries. There is an alternative that gives you
peace of mind. Private home management
saves you money AND time- without long term
contracts, real estate agents and more
headaches. Mark is an expert, author, and
educator with years of experience watching
empty homes. There is no pressure, he is your
personal consultant to guide you to the best
decision.
Relax, knowing you have:
What our clients say:

• Extra time to make the best decision- Sell it? Keep it? Rent it?

• An on-site expert to call anytime with questions or requests
• Save storage fees, protect against insurance losses

-- 511 of 625 --

Hi, this is Mark Mehling, author of "Leaving Your Home-ALONE", the
leading reference guide for absentee homeowners like you.
DID YOU COME HOME
TO A RUSlY DRYER?
Want to know the secret why?
You see, what is happening is very typical here in Florida. In my
new book, due out next summer called "Leaving your CONDO-ALONE" ,
I discuss just this problem. But I will let you in on a little section of the book
before it comes out for publication.
Did you come home from your wonderful summer up North only to find
water, or worse, rust, in the dryer in your beautiful Bella Vista condo? What
a shock! How aggravating! Did your clothes get rust stains? Will you
need to replace the dryer because the drum inside is now a mess? Are
other parts getting rusty, guaranteeing service calls, early failure, and
more surprises and expenses?
You have worked hard to afford the luxury of Bella Vista and don't need
these hassles and aggravations that eat up your time. Sitting around, waiting
for appliance people who promise to be there at 9 AM and then show up
promptly at 1130. You could have been walking on the beach but your day
was completely wasted. What is your time worth?
WARNING: A little Science here.
From the Draft copy:
"When you leave your condo empty for months at a time, (or you have an
untrained/inexperienced 'once a month' home watch service), the air inside your
closed dryer equalizes with the outside air through the vent hose which normally
exhausts the hot air. That means the super-humid hot air outside equalizes with the air
in the dryer drum. The cooler air in the condo keeps the metal drum cooler than the
humid air inside your dryer and you get (scientific info here!) the 'tee glass effect. ' Just
as a tea glass develops moisture, or condensates, on the warmer side of the ice cold
drink glass, the moisture condensates on the warm side of the dryer drum- inside.
This moisture can cause rust, ruin your nice clothes, cause early failure of the
appliance, and make you the repairman's best customer.
How can you prevent this from happening? You probably don't see this happening
when you are living in the unit, do you? That's because you routinely use the
appliance. If you ensure that the appliance is run for at least 10 minutes every other
512
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 512 of 625 --

week, you will never have the problem. The heat from the dryer will remove any moist
air inside the drum and exhaust it. Once the drum is dry, ensure the door is left open to
allow lower humidity condo air to circulate inside the drum. This solution eliminates
inline doors, flappers, and 'socks' that can clog from dryer dust or need changing at
regular intervals, too." (I go on to describe how to keep the dryer light from burning out but
that is for another time.)
Now this solution does require you ensure these three critical elements:
./ the humidity is kept low in the condo while you are gone
./ someone is checking on the property more than once a month
./ they use a tailored checklist that ensures this check is done each time
My private home management clients do not have this problem. Why? Very simply because
our service ensures this doesn't happen. We know what the issue is based on years of
experience and make it a 'non-issue' for our clients. Our exclusive 101 point checklist is
tailored by an author, educator, and expert to match YOUR individual unit. With the
precision of a commercial pilot, our checklists eliminate hundreds of potential problems just
like this one.
If you would like to stop worrying about your investment, stop coming home to surprises, and
stop being the embarassed by problems you could have solved, give me a call.
Want an eye popping experience? You can get an ABSOLUTELY FREE, no-obligation "WOW,
I never knew THAT!" survey of potential problems in your condo just for being a Bella Vista
Owner. It is my gift to you to celebrate the beginning of our 5th year in business. You get the
results and choose how you want to handle any issues, if at all. There is absolutely no
obligation- nothing will be sold, and I will even show you how to fix anything we find.
But hurry, this offer ends November 1st, 2010.
For more information, you can visit us online at www.theHomeWatchGuy.com or via
email at mark@homewatchvalet.com or call my personal cell at 386-212-6770. We are also
available toll-free at 888- HW -VALET (888-498-2538) for your convenience.
Mark the HomeWatchGuy

Dear Mr. XXXXXXXXXXX
Quite frankly, the HomeWatchValet Club is not for everyone. And not everyone who applies for
Membership is approved.
However, because we believe that you will benefit from Membership, I've enclosed a special invitation
for you to apply for the most respected and prestigious home management service available to people
who travel extensively or snowbird.
The HomeWatchValet Club is the perfect example of the old adage, "You get what you pay/or."
For example, you get a truly impressive array of extra privileges, all designed for your convenience and
Peace of Mind:
-Privacy. A single personal Valet will be the one person routinely in your home. No worries
about the neighbors (or their kids) routing around in your things while you are gone.
-Your Own Valet. A single point of contact, your personal valet, not some generic phone
number or whoever happens to answer the phone. Your Valet, an individual you will meet
and know personally, will provide you unparalleled service. He or she will be able to handle
your smallest worry or concern. They are accountable to me, so you will also receive my
personal cell number to immediately resolve an issue.
-Premium Service/limited Enrollment. We only accept a limited number of clients to ensure
this premium service exceeds our Clubmember's expectations. No request is burdensome,
and you never impose.
-Trustworthy/Reliable Service. Scheduled visits to your empty property by your licensed,
bonded, and trained Valet. Unlike maids, pet sitters, or even friends and neighbors, your
Valet is part of a proven system that has saved others thousands of dollars in heartburn and
headaches, allowing you to spend your time doing things you love, like golf bridge, or
philanthropy.
-Membership bonuses for the season including free 'Everything but the Porcelain' Toilet
Warranty, initiatiation fee discounts, and 'Dinner on Us' when you return. Your Valet will
have complete information on each of these during your initial meeting.
-Free Time. Enjoy the freedom of a totally prepared residence- no bugs, no problems,
everything prepared for your arrival. Plan on unpacking- everything else is handled by your
Valet to ensure you don't miss your tee time!
Extras like these only begin to tell the story of HomeWatchValet's attention to detail, care for your
lifestyle, and personal convenience. You will also enjoy:

-- 517 of 625 --

DEFINITIONS:
The COMPANY should have enough insurance to cover the potential risk to your
home. $100,000 can disappear quickly if the screw up and mold enters your
home. Itemized electronic bills, a website, and handling credit cards is what any
professional business invests to provide customer service to their client. Without
these, who knows what the rest of their care will look like in your absence.
A PROFESSIONAL knows that time is money- yours and his. He should be
respectful of your time, be organized when he comes, and be prepared in
advanced. A logo'd shirt indicates a commitment to the business. Car signs
should be visible in case your neighbors wonder who is going into your home
when you are gone. Remember that a raggedy looking person with a vehicle
that scares the neighbors is probably taking care of your property the same
way. As far as a service agreement or contract, remember Roy Rogers
famous saying "A verbal contract isn't worth the paper it's written on."
Professional tools includes a high grade humidity sensor, temperature
sensors, certified smoke to test smoke alarms, etc. A plan for your home
should include a minimum of two visits per month using checklists
customized for your specific property.
A KNOWLEDGEABLE Company or individual knows exactly what happens to
the homeowners Insurance policy after the property has been vacant for 30
days. There should also be a simple easy to understand mitigation plan for
damage when and if it does occur. What will they do for a broken window?
Leaking roof? Broken water line? Hurricane or tornado?
Actual MANAGEMENT OF THE HOUSE is the most critical area other than
the Company itself. An outside sign, similar to those used by a security
company, should have a toll free number to call if anyone sees a problem-
like your friends or neighbors or even someone walking a dog. Without a
sign, who would they call when you are unavailable? Free Grocery shopping
makes your home-coming easier. Lost keys could be an issue and you need
to know who is going to pay to re-key ALL YOUR LOCKS should this occur.
Some services include visits after major storms but be sure to ask the
definition for 'major storm'.
OTHER SERVICES should be available to make your arrival easier. Why
should you have to be in the property all days waiting for carpet cleaning,
window cleaners, or pressure washers? Besides, if there is a leak or other
damage, the service will need these people to help with the cleanup. They need
to guarantee the work of anyone they hire, instead of you being saddled with
problems when you come home. Specialized home plans should be available
for year round management, 'for sale' properties, etc to demonstrate this is a
thriving, reliable, trusted by others service. If you haven't done an Insurance
Accepted Home Inventory, the service should offer a professional service or a
DIY kit at a reasonable rate.

snowbird houses, For Sale homes,_p-ro'bate p-rop-erties, inherited resJidencesJ homes with owner lin ass,isted lliivingc__-.
If your home is unoccupied when you head up North, your
Home Owner's Insurance DECREASES and can be canceled.)k
~ But there is a solution519
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 519 of 625 --

Port Orange, Florida/ Homeowner's have
been hit with skyrocketing insurance
premiums, and many insurers are moving out.
Those that remain easily terminate policies
when they see additional risk ofloss.
A local author, recognized as one of
America's Premier Experts®, is on a
mission to save homeowners from canceled
insurance policies. "Snowbirds leaving homes
behind can have their insurance terminated"
he warns. Why? Simply because the unwary
owner leaves the property unoccupied for
more than 30 days.
Mark Mehling, author of the book
"Leaving Yom' Home-ALONE" is a
pioneer in the field of private home
management. We asked him some questions:
"Why haven't I heard this before?
Mehling: "Most agents don't want to tell
their customers that their coverage
DECREASES when they leave and their
insurance could be cancelled. So they just
hope nothing happens and the home office
doesn't ask questions."
Florida insurance is harder to get
every day, can they really terminate
my policy?
Mehling: "Absolutely, unless you have a
licensed and insured home watch service that
provides you with a 'due diligence' statement,
many companies are more than happy to
lower their risk by terminating even long term
customers. Ignorance is no longer accepted."
What about having my house cleaner
or neighbors watch the house?
Mehling: "Maids, pet sitters, and neighbors
are not licensed/insured home watch
businesses and do not qualify by your home
owner's insurance standards. So your
insurance can be canceled. Besides, would you'
let your bouse cleaner manage your half
million dollar retirement portfolio for six
months while you are gone? Of course not,
you use a professional for that, and your
house deserves the same because it is just as
valuable."
HomeWotch'alet
1866 Seclusion Dr

You're probably wondering
hy would someone
d me headache pills?"
"
se
· Leslie,
· g here and these are
om doesn'f need
_...__o_e- let me explain. Mark Mehling, author
of the book "Leaving
Your Home-ALONE"
Leslie, Are you like my Mom?
mother is a wonderful woman, who put up with some of the most
- credib'le stunts from yours truly. On top of all the laundry, dishes and
cooking that kept my three brothers and me ready for anything growing up- I
never realized everything she did and what a blessing it was until I moved
out on my own.
But my mother never knew the intricacies of running a household from a
maintenance and BILL PAYING point of view. And everything seemed to
en Dad was gone. While Mom,could handle the everyday issues, it all depended on
a ces, vehicles, air conditioner WORKING right and the bills getting paid. She had no
- ea wbat to do about the car, changing the oil. car batteries. garage door openers, air
·oners,_garbage disposals, lawn care, insect control, etc. The appliances were
posed to work!! There weren't SUPPOSEDto be BUGS!! Sorting and paying all THOSE
S was quite overwhelming. "Wait until Dad gets home" kept her going for years. But when
my Father wasn't there, she was completely at a loss as to what to do-these problems just
gave her a HUGE HEADACHE (thus the pills you have above)!!! But it wasn't her fault.
Bills need paying, Properties need maintenance
Organizing bills, getting help with 'honey-do's', and everyday care of your property means
items need constant minor tweaking to make sure they last longer. Appliances need
maintenance, cars need oil changes, tire rotations, and routine servlcinq, lawns need spraying,
bug problems need attention. The list was endless and just added stress to my Mom. That got
me to thinking. Mom needed ~
Call TOLL FREE from anywhere- 888.HW.VALET (888.498.2538)

SUPER SIMPLE MARKETING TRICK
Will Increase the Lifetime Value of Every Customer, Get More
Referrals, and Take Less than an Hour a Month to Implement!
TRUE STORY - 18 months ago I met a Personal Injury Attorney from Atlanta who wanted to mail out a
monthly print newsletter to just over 3,000 former clients and Chiropractors that have practices in his area.
He told me they currently get few referrals and almost no repeat clients, even though he knew for sure his
clients were very happy with his service.
So I said, “If people are happy with you and your firm, why are they not referring and/or doing business with
you again?” And, very matter of fact, he replied, “Because they can’t remember my name.” Recently he had
done a survey of 200 former clients who had won or settled a case with his firm between 6 and 18 months
ago, and ONLY 26 of them knew who his firm was when they called. When they can’t remember your name,
it is hard for them to refer friends and family to do business with you again.
Fast forward 18 months, The Newsletter Pro has now been creating, printing, and mailing a monthly
newsletter to all the firm’s former clients. When this same attorney looked at his referrals and repeat
business for the previous 12 months, he found that 32% of his new clients are now repeat clients or referred
clients from former clients. His newsletter is now the single highest ROI marketing piece for the firm.
This is just one of dozens of stories we have from clients who have implemented a monthly print newsletter
only to turn around and discover that it’s the single best piece of marketing in their arsenal.
You already know that a print newsletter is a must for any business, you may even be sending a generic
newsletter now, but you’ve always wanted to send a custom newsletter. At The Newsletter Pro we know how
to create custom newsletters that get results. Our newsletters get opened and read, increase the lifetime
value of your customers, help you get more referrals, and only requires one hour or less a month of your
input - but sounds like you wrote the whole thing!
To help you get started with your own newsletter, I have put
together our best offer EVER! If you get started with newsletters for
your company by Halloween, you’ll receive three very special treats:
Treat 1 : $500 gift certificate good towards your first month’s newsletter creation and printing.
Treat 2 : FREE 5 day 4 night cruise for two. One of the biggest reasons stated as to why
someone wants to become an entrepreneur, is the ability to take great vacations. We want to help with
that goal by giving you a gift certificate towards a free cruise!
Treat 3 : No Postage Increase In 2014 - The post office recently requested a minimum of a $0.03
increase in postage from the governing body. When you get started with newsletters, we will lock in the
current postage rate for all of 2014. While three cents doesn’t sound like a lot, it can add up quickly when
you are mailing 500 to a few thousand newsletters per month.
524
www.getwsodownload.com

team know and they will happily sheculde a call with me or my right hand woman, Kelly, and we will get all
your questions answered.
Shaun Buck

-- 525 of 625 --

Ninja Newsletter Technique gets a 24% response rate and makes
your printed newsletter go viral!
How would you like to be able to make sure your clients are actively engaged in your
newsletter and have proof of it?
Want a way to get a 24% response to your monthly list?
How would you like to have your newsletter actually go viral?
We wanted a way to make our newsletter readers actively engage with us when they read the newsletter instead of
just passively reading it and then throwing it away. We wanted a way to make it more fun and have our clients
desire our newsletters every month. So we put a “Word Find” activity into our newsletter, but instead of using
someone else’s “Word Find” we made our own. We used MS Excel and simply put the words into it ourselves, but
our secret ninja technique was to use words associated with our product. So this teaches our clients about the
terminology of the industry as well as giving them a good feeling about our services. We have a 24% rate of
engagement with this activity and each month we change it to reflect what is going on with us. We also give them a
reward for responding to it. We typically use gift cards that we get from reward points from our vendors and
creditors so there is only a minimal cost to us to do this for our clients.
There are some major advantages we get when our client fills in the information. One of them is that we get updated
contact information and it also lets us know they are still actively involved with us. Another is that we get an email
address where we may have not had one. And lastly, and this one is huge – The newsletter grows our list. You see
when our client fills in the word find and goes to the fax machine, their co-workers want to find out what the
excitement is all about and so they start to talk about getting a $10.00 gift card from one of their vendors. So as
soon as the first fax is sent to us, the other employee grabs the word find, scratches out the other persons name
and fills it in for himself and then sends us another fax. So now we have a newsletter that becomes viral and we
get to grow our client contact list.
526
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 526 of 625 --

SERVICE 	FROM THE NO B.S. MARKETING LETTER
Bob & Art Professional Cleaning – Letter
Bob & Art 	LOGO
May 27, 20XX
Dan Kennedy
5818 N. 7th Street, Suite 103
Phoenix, Arizona 85014
Dear Dan,
Bob 	Art
Ceccarelli 	McCarthy
Photo 	Photo
I'm writing to let you know how we are doing with our "get new customers" campaign. 	You
will recall that we started out with 2350 names in an affluent neighborhood compiled from a
street directory. We decided to send 800 postcards (#1 enclosed) as a test. 5 people called and
placed orders- a 0.625% response. Initially, I was a little disappointed. 	I was hoping for 1%.
However, the size of their orders was such that we actually made a small profit. So we mailed
the rest of the list. I was delighted with the unexpected higher response of 0.85%.
We have done direct mail in various formats many times before. I've gotten higher response
rates. But this mailing ferreted out more customers that will become repeat buyers. How do I
know? Because they fit our profile of those who buy frequently- monies, large homes with lots
of wall-to-wall carpeting, and have children or pets to quickly re-soil their carpeting.
What we haven't done before, what your newsletter inspired us to try, is sequential mailings to
the same list. What would happen if we closely followed up with another mailer?
A couple of weeks later we mailed another 765 (800 less nixies less respondents) postcards (#2
enclosed) to the test portion of our list. We immediately got 4 more calls. But 2 were inquiries
that our secretary, Betty, could not sell. Another person placed an order but later called back and
cancelled. 	The 4'h person turned out to be a good new customer. A few days later, 2 more
people called - both real nice jobs. Then another person- who got both postcards, saw our truck
at her neighbors house and came over after we left to inspect our work - called. Some people are
unbelievably hard to sell, aren't they?
Interestingly, of the 4 people who were motivated by the 2nd postcard to purchase our services, 2
knew the customers I named in my copy, a third heard about us before, and the 4th is the one who came
over and inspected our work. Tells you something, doesn’t it?
527
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 527 of 625 --

The response rate to postcard #2 sent to the test portion of our list is about 0.5% or about break-
even. The way I figure it is like this: the total dollars made were about the same as cost of
marketing plus our cost to provide the service.
Overall, we got 22 new customers and made a couple hundred dollars to boot. Not bad, eh?
So what happened when I mailed the second card to the rest of the list? Well, I never mailed the
second card to the remaining names.
This year got off to a very slow start. Usually we start booking residential carpet cleaning
toward the end of March. By April, we are usually booking at a fairly good clip. But this year
was different. We had 17 major snow storms this past winter. There was snow on the ground
well into April. I think that is the reason bookings were sluggish. At any rate, once the weather
broke, the phone rang off the wall. Good news, yes, but I had to back off on the marketing.
What now? We have to shift gears. The 22 new customers will get our periodic customer list
mailers which I also am going to send to the 761 prospects on our test list. I'm going to
experiment with a concept I call an "information card" (#3 enclosed). I've experimented with
newsletters but found the returns, although good, weren't worth the effort, that is, they were no
better than the results from a simple postcard. Nevertheless, I do think the idea of providing our
customers with useful information has merit. So I'm going to try sending a little useful cleaning
related information and a special offer on a postcard to our customers and 761 test prospects
every month or so and see what happens. If time permits, and if enough prospects respond, I'll
send the info cards to the remainder of our prospect list also.
Hey, Dan, I'm getting fantastic value from my subscription. Anyone who is active in marketing
and is not a subscriber is missing out on rock-solid info. Too bad for them. Keep up the good
work. I recommend you whenever I can.
Warmest Regards,
Bob Ceccarelli
528
www.getwsodownload.com

Dear Friend,
For the past 10 years, my partner and I have
given outrageously good service to your
neighbors. 	Understandably, we've had to do
very little paid advertising because word-of-
mouth advertising kept both of our trucks
going full-time.
But this past fall, my partner's son David
decided to join our business. 	Let me tell you,
David is as thorough and meticulous as we are. I
would not bother writing you otherwise. At any
rate, to keep David busy this month, we have to
get more customers.
So here is my offer. 	Have us clean your
carpets before the end of April 20XX and I'll
pay half your bill. You will save SO%.
Incidentally, our price quotes are never
inflated and always include everything. There
are no hidden costs.
Oh, by-the-way, please call for references. I got
your address from a street directory. I chose
addresses near our customers' homes. We will
give you the names of people who live only a
few doors away. Ask them about our service.
Then call us with full confidence that you are
getting the best service at the lowest price
possible.
But don't delay. This offer positively ends April
30,20XX. 	Our secretary, Betty, will gladly
answer your questions and, if you wish,
schedule an appointment for you. 	Why not call
now? Our phone numbers (both numbers ring at
our Factoryville office) are on the other side of
this post card.
Sincerely,
Bob Ceccarelli

back.

Why Hot, Humid Summer Days
"Reactivate" Pet Odor Problems
Urine deposits are hygroscopic. That means they
absorb moisture from the air. There is much more
moisture present in the air on hot,
we thoroughly clean the carpeting. Then we treat the
affected areas with Outright Pet Odor Eliminator, a
natural product that uses friendly bacteria to
“consume" the contaminates trapped in the carpet
backing. Outright leaves no residue, no odor, no
nothing - other than sparkling clean, spring fresh
carpets. It is nature's way of cleaning up.
humid days. So the deposits
become more moist and thus give
off more odor.
How We Eliminate
Pet Odors From Carpets
We use moisture sensors to
precisely locate urine deposits.
Since the deposits are
hygroscopic, they never really dry
out. They will trigger our
moisture sensors months, even
years, after the last "accident."
After defining the areas affected,
Picture of Product
Caption:
Outright is also
available for do-it-
yourself use through
Bob & Art
How Much Does Your
Pet Odor Removal Service Cost?
It depends on the level of contamination. Call
and we will inspect your problem. Then we
will give you a written estimate detailing the
steps necessary and the cost. There is no charge
for the inspection or the estimate. We will give
you a one year guarantee against
recurring odor if you go with our
recommended service. For obvious reasons, the
guarantee is valid only if you no longer have
pets in your home. Call 836-5736 or
348- I 006. Thank you.

SERVICE 	FROM THE NO B.S. MARKETING LETTER
"Look For Our Dad" Carpet Cleaning Ad/FSI/Vai-Pak®/MoneyMailer®
SAMPLE OF THE MONTH
IC Member Blake Miller is doing some first-rate work with his Chem-Dry Franchise- here in this
Issue, I've duplicated his full-page MoneyMailer® insert and my comments about it* and photos
showing his take-one sign and take-one van box. (Ad follows.) He's doing everything he can think of
to generate business! Now here's ANOTHER strategy I suggested to him, that might interest any to-
consumer service provider, with a bit of grunt work. Blake can use the Criss-Cross Street Directory,
available free at the library, look up his customer's address, get the addresses next door and across the
street, get the family names, and send them a "We Just Cleaned Your Neighbor, John & Mary Smith's
Carpets - Maybe You Saw Our Van And Sign There. Now We Have A Great Offer For You, Too"
letter, along with the flyer. This is a direct sales industry fundamental: 	every customer instantly
yields five prospects: the neighbors on both sides plus the three directly across the street. Since a good
sales pro'll close (at least) one out of five, this really means that every sale births another.
*The flyer is full color, so the copy here probably isn't very good, but good enuff! Here's what I told
them, in my Critique: "The flyer is GREAT. I love the "Look For Our Dad"... But I do have TWO
"Big Ideas" for you. Idea #1: Use the BACK! You're paying for a whole billboard, then using half ...
paper's expensive, ink's cheap. Put a sales letter, testimonials, something(s) on the back. (One color
ink ok.) Maybe a letter from the kids, signed by each of them in bad print, their real sigs. And #2: I'd
invest in a different flyer for Realtors® than everybody else. I think telling other consumers that
certain people are getting 18% off is a problem."
Now, a few more comments, for everybody's edification: 	in a media like MoneyMailer®, multiple
coupons almost always out pull a single coupon, so they've done the right thing here. In a scam-
riddled business, they have correctly emphasized their strong, straightforward guarantee. (In the
actual flyer, the box's border and headline is red, body copy black, on white.) Guarantee would be
strengthened if signed by Blake and Terri. They've also tackled the credibility issue with "world's
largest." Most of the copy is good. The subheads are good, but might be slightly improved - for
egs. GUARANTEED LOWEST PRICING EVERY TIME.
This is an excellent use of the coupon-pack media. And speaking of which, it is my opinion that
MoneyMailer® gives many advertisers THE best bang for the buck. Here's why: 90% of all recipients
open and "browse" through the pack. There is no other type of mailing you can send and get anywhere
near 90% of it opened. This is an enormous advantage. Second, most advertisers using
MoneyMailer® get at least 50 responses per 10,000 ... at a cost of less than $10 each... and it's very
tough to match those economics any other way.
*Reprinted: 	No B.S. Marketing Newsletter, September
532
www.getwsodownload.com

From Ron Ipach:
FROM RON IPACH
This is the 3-step campaign that our clients use to attract new customers to their shops.
The 'hook' is the coded message that requires the reader to bring in the letter or postcard and place it
under the special decoder at the repair shop in order to reveal their prize.
Our clients have seen as much as an 11% response to this campaign.

SERVICE
Service Business Customer Newsletter
FROM RON IPACH
From Ron Ipach:
This is a copy of a newsletter that we customize for our clients that kicks butt for several reasons:
• 	It doesn't focus on just one topic! What the business owner 'does' for a living is NOT
interesting to the reader Just imagine a pest control owner sending out a newsletter all
about creepy crawly bugs - yuk!) If it bores the recipient, future issues will be thrown
away unread. There is always something in it that everyone can relate to.
• 	It's sent out monthly to ensure 'top of the mind' awareness
• 	It's a great delivery system for offers - it's a welcome break from the 'buy from me'
messages that their customers have become used to
• 	Best of all, the newsletter is done for the business owner - freeing up dozens of hours of
marketing work each month

www.getwsodownload.com

July F~ELtor 2008
A Free Monthly Newsletter From Your Friends At CinRon Marketing Group
Celebrating Independence
Day At Home
Traffic is heavy. parks are crowded and friends would
rather celebrate at your place.
The Fourth ofJuly is a great day to stay home. The time
is right because your spring work is done. The deck, patio
and yard should be in great shape for all to see. They provide
the backdrop for an enjoyable day.
\Vhether you plan a barbecue for family and a few others
or a real party outdoors, a few decorations can add color to
the festivities. Generally. they are inexpensive theme pieces
available at party stores. And you could ask guests to wear
red, white, and blue.
Food, music. games and fireworks are basic.
No need to break
the bank buying
steaks and fancy
foods. Everyone likes
hamburgers and hot
dogs. For a simple
party. add potato
chips, beans, a salad
or two. fresh finger
vegetables with veggie dip, a dessert and plenty of cool
drinks.
Or try a firec-racker salad with strawberries ·in red gelatin,
whipped cream on top1 and blueberries on the whipped cream
for red, white and blue. Poke in a celery stick.
Provide outdoor activities. After dark, play safe with the
fireworks.

Put Pennies And Nickels Back Into
Circulation, Please!
Because of high copper prices, it now costs the U.S. Mint
1.3 cents to make a penny and 7.7 cents for a nickeL
[tis estimated that there are some 150 billion coins going
unused. That's about $90 per house standing in jars and loose
in drawers and sofa cushions.
According to the Sustainable Research 	-. 	( J>--
Group, there would be big environmental \ 	~~ 	-
benefits if just l 0 percent of donnant 	~
coins were re-circulated. 	l~"' 	\Water savings at the mint would 	- 	+
be equal to abo~t 82 million showers. 	~
Energy savings could light 4.1 million
bulbs for 1,000 hours each C02 emissions equal to those from
12,619 cars would be kept out of the atmosphere.
Check The Back Seat Before
Leaving The Car
Certainly you would never intentionally leave a baby, child
or pet locked in the car when the weather is hot, or anvtime.
Within I 0 minutes, the temperature in a car can cause.heat
stroke.
But you could be very busy or preoccupied. A sleeping
child in the back seat could be forgotten. The CDC recommends
placing a stuffed animal on the front seat to help you remember.
Always check to see that eve1yone is out of the car before
leaving it.
Leave pets at home. Move your pet into the air conditioned
house or at least move them into the shade. Be sure there is
plenty of water available.
Do You Have A Question About Car
Maintenance That You Want Answered?
We love to hear from all of our good friends and clients who
enjoy reading our monthly newsletter. If you have a question
about anything related to your vehicle, feel free to give us a
call at 800-797-0537 ext 206 or email us at
newsletter@cinron.com. www.cinron.com.

Thank You! 	Thank You! 	Thank You!
Special thanks go out to all of our clients and friends who
graciously referred our shop to their friends and neighbors last
month! Our business is built based on the positive comments
and referrals from people just like you. We just couldn't do it

Julie Nixon
Professor's 'The Last Lecture' Tells
A Story About How To Live
lt's not unusual for retiring professors to give a last lecture.
When Carnegie Mellon University!s Randy Pausch gave his. he
wasn't retiring. He was dying of pancreatic cancer.
His now famous lecture was viewed by millions on
YouTube. It inspired many to follow Pausch's advice about
what is important in life. Now he has a book The Last Lecture
(Hyperion), with co-author Jeffrey Zaslow.
The father of three small children. Pausch said in a USA
Today interview. "I was trying to put myself in a bottle that
would one day wash up on the beach for my children." From
his lecture, Achieving Your Childhood Dreams, here are a few
of his points about life:
* Never underestimate the importance of having fun. I'm
dying and I'm having fun every day because there's no other
way to play it.
* Experience is what you ge-t -when you didn't get what you
wanted.
*No one is pure eviL Find the best in everybody. Wait
long enough and people will surprise you.
* It's not about achieving your dreams but about living
your life. If you live the right way, the dreams will come.
*We can't change the cards we're dealt, just how we play
the hand.
* Jn an interview with amazon.com. Pausch says the thing
he learned as he grew older was that you can't get anywhere
without help. Ask yourself: What kind of person do people
want to help'? Then you'll know the answer to: What kind of
person should I try to be?
* Healing what other people say about you is important to
your success and happiness.
His book is humorous while allowing him to tell of his life
experiences and the lessons he learned. For one: The secret of
winning the stuffed animal on the midway, like most anything
else, is sheer persistence. But it helps to have long arms and
discretionary income.
Good Advice For College Freshmen
And Their Parents
School will start in a few \Veeks, but it's not too late to help
your college freshman avoid poor choices. Student
development offices give this advice:
v' 	Give yourself a break when signing up for classes. Don't
overload your schedule. Get used to social and academic
demands on your time.
Buy the books. You will save money by buying used
books. but new books can be sold later. A void book
sharing. Always have a book of your own.
Plan to study at least an hour for each hour in class. Some
courses require more. lt's not like high school.
Check your email at least once a day so you won't miss
notices from professors or campus~ wide messages.
A void committing a certain number of hours to an outside
job until after the first week of classes. By then you'll have
a better idea about how much you can do and still keep up
with your studies.
./ 	Ask for help when you need it. There are math and \.Vriting
labs, peer tutoring, and workshops. Or talk with your
professor during office hours. Many freshman need extra
help.
Keep socializing in balance with study requirements.
Focusing completely on study can create a lonely or
depressing college experience. Make time to socialize and
study.
Stay positive. You can do it.
Don't feel pressured to choose a career in your freshman
year .
./ 	Take time to discover your strengths and where your true
interests and talents lie. Consider the possibilities. A visit
to the campus career office can help. But sign up for some
offbeat classes just because they interest you. Never can
tel! where you will find inspiration.
Dettling For The Tax Rebates
The National Retail Foundation estimates that purchases
generated from tax rebate checks will come to $42.9 billion.
Big-box chains and other retailers want a piece of the action
and are wi IIing to pay for it.
Sears Holdings (Sears. Kmart. Lands' End and others) is

July Fourth Potato Salad
./ 	2 cups boiled red potatoes with skin left on
./ 	2 cups of boiled small white potatoes with skin left on
../ 	2 hardboiled eggs cut in small pieces
1 tablespoon celery seed
l/2 cup blue cheese crumbles
./ 	3 tablespoons light mayonnaise
l/2 cup natural yogurt
1 tablespoon sugar
./ 	5 tablespoons horseradish
./ 	6 tablespoons chipotle mustard
1 tablespoon of white vinegar
l teaspoon fresh ground pepper
112 teaspoon of salt
1/2 cup chives or bacon
bits for garnish
Mix together and enjoy'

Life's Little Truths
;;p 	Age is a high price to pay for maturity.
® 	Mv idea of housework: Sweep the room with a glance.
1$- 	N~t one shred of evidence supports the notion that life is
serious.
#II 	If you look like your passport picture, you probably need
the trip,
~;~~ Artificial intelligence is no match for natural stupidity.
Itt 	No husband has ever been shot while doing the dishes.
0 	Opportunities always look bigger going than coming.
$' 	Junk is something you've kept for years and throw away
three weeks before you need it.
t> 	It is easier to get forgiveness than permission .
~ 	Experience is wonderful. It enables you to recognize a
mistake when you make it.
'iW 	By the time you can make ends meet, they move the ends.
(; 	Thou shaH not weigh more than thy refrigerator.
~ 	Someone who thinks logically is a nice contrast to the real
world.

COPYRIGHT FREE
Photo
Dear Neighbor,
For Richfield Neighbor:
A FREE <Insert Gift/Widget>
is
reserved
for YOU --- All I ask in return is a chance to say "hello."
My name is Sam Smith, and I am an Auto Sales Professional with # years'
experience, at Quality Dealership Inc., specializing in servicing my neighbors in
Richfield. (My Personal Brochure is enclosed.)
Many of your neighbors are already my clients. In fact, as you drive down your
street, it's a very safe bet that some of your neighbors' garages and driveways contain a
new or pre-owned automobile that I put there. Now I've set a simple goal of at least
personally meeting and shaking hands with EVERY Richfield resident.
For that reason, I've had a very special <insert widget> prepared as my FREE
GOODWILL GIFT for you and your family, and I'd like to drop it by, at your
convenience.
I promise not to try and "sell" you a thing!
Just call me and let's set a time when I can swing by, drop off your gift, shake
hands and say hello.
Hopefully, when the time comes for you to make a change in automobiles, you'll
remember me and give me the opportunity of being of service.
Thanks,
SAM SMITH
000/000-0000
PS: A page of comments about me from some of my clients is also enclosed.

Dear Richfield Neighbor,
IS THERE ANY REAL
DIFFERENCE
BETWEEN One Automobile
Salesperson And Another?
YOUR FREE GIFT IS WAITING.
A couple weeks ago, I sent you a brief letter, introducing myself. 	You may have
no need for a new car now, but someday you, a family member or a friend certainly will,
and I simply hope, then, you'll give me an opportunity of being of service.
In the meantime, just so you and I can get acquainted, I've had a special <Insert
Widget> prepared as a free goodwill gift, and I have yours, reserved in your name,
waiting for you right here on my desk. There's no cost, no obligation – I'd just
appreciate being able to drop off this gift and say hello.
And, for your future reference, I would like to point out that there are huge
differences between automobile salespeople. 	A brief list of the ways my clients say I
differ from most is enclosed.
Please give me a call.
Thanks,
SAM SMITH

really is amazing!

Sam Smith

Looking to sell your home? Don’t miss this Free Report Packed with sure fire profit-boosting strategies:
“The 9 Hidden Secrets For Selling Your
Home FAST While Getting Top Dollar”
As a homeowner, chances are you’ve been given advice or read ideas about what you should (and shouldn’t) do to get
top dollar for your house or make a fast sale.
The problem is, "fast sale" and "top dollar" don’t often go together. In fact, many realtors will tell you that if you want to
sell fast, you should list your home below market value. But I’m here to tell you that’s not the only way.
My name’s Jan Realtor. After 25 years as a top-producing Realtor, I’ve perfected a formula of nine things you can do to
not only get top dollar for your home, but to also sell your home much faster than the average transaction time. In fact,
many of my clients who use these nine secrets have sold their home in as little as 30 days.
Inside my Free Report “9 Hidden Secrets For Selling Your Home FAST While Getting Top Dollar,” I’ll reveal:
• 	One easy way to instantly increase the value of your home—that won’t cost you a dime.
• 	The color that makes more people want to look at your house—and three different ways to incorporate it if you
aren’t currently using it.
• 	The real checklist for getting your house ready to sell. (Miss one of these items and you instantly decrease the
market value of your home).
• 	What you should do to give your home a “Wow” effect when showing.
• 	How to draw more attention to your house over and above all other houses that are competing with yours for
the sale.
• 	What you should know about the “behind the scenes” things your realtor is (or isn’t) doing. If you want top
dollar AND to sell FAST, then your realtor must be doing all of these.
• 	And much more…
Warning: You won’t find this formula on any other website or offered by any other realtor. Plus, because of the secrets
revealed, this may only be offered for a short time. Download your FREE report, “The 9 Hidden Secrets For Selling Your
Home FAST While Getting Top Dollar” NOW, while you still can.

Dear Friend,
To My Richfield Friends:
WHO DO YOU KNOW
I Might Be Of Service To
THIS MONTH?
Last month, I received REFERRALS from a number of my Richfield friends, and,
as a result, helped five people acquire new cars. (Their pictures with their new cars are
all on the enclosed "Happy Page.") I gave each of those who referred a $25.00 GIFT
CERTIFICATE from A&J's Gourmet Food Markets as my “thank you”, although they
had no idea they were getting it ahead of time.
Now they've called to thank me for introducing them to A&J's. 	(Try and find
olives stuffed with crabmeat anywhere else in Indy!)
Anyway, here's the point – I'd like to introduce you to A&J's too. And, frankly, I
NEED to sell more cars THIS MONTH. In fact, I'm involved in a little "bet" with
another salesman here at Quality, and I'm going to be, pardon the expression, "wheeling
and dealing" to win that bet. So, if you refer a new client to me this month, a $25.00
A&J's Gift Certificate will arrive in your mailbox.
I've enclosed a few business cards, Reply Cards, and Sales Sheets, and I hope
you 'II pass them along to anybody who might like a great deal on a car this month.
Thanks,
Sam Smith

name to the Indianapolis Food Bank too for each new client referred this month.

NEW BEGINNINGS!
Dear Friend,
HAPPY NEW YEAR! I hope this proves to be a wonderful new year for you and your
family. Maybe you'd like to start the new year off with
If so, January’s the right time to buy. Why? Because it is, historically, a "slow" month
for the car business. I'll need your help to get my new year off to a fast start, and I'm able
to offer some special discounts and incentives to get you through the snow, down to the
showroom:

model year and management

different brand new 20_
automobiles that just have not
moved and MUST move now .
Some are in colors that haven't
Same Smith
been popular (but you might love them ).

no power windows , now power seats.

given the job of moving these cars FAST.

we've offered on these cars up to now
from our previous ads and price stickers,
and then you and I can know no less than
$1,000.00 as much as $5,000.00 off. You
see, the boss has a gun against his head.
He has to make room for the incoming
models. He cannot keep
paying the interest to keep these cars
around. So come in and see for yourself
what incredible bargains there can be
during "The Boss Has A Gun Against His
Head Days."

Mr. and Mrs. John smith
1111 Meadowbrook
Hudson, Ohio
Dear Mr. and Mrs. Smith,
From The Hudson
Community Specialists
DON'T SETTLE FOR EMPTY PROMISES AGAIN. ALL REALTORS ARE NOT
THE SAME. 	HAVING A REALTOR WHO SPECIALIZES IN HUDSON DOES
MAKE A DIFFERENCE.
This morning, my daily check of the Hudson community records showed that
your listing agreement has expired- and your home has NOT been sold as you had
hoped.
No doubt, you will receive several phone calls in the next few days from other
real estate salespeople, asking for appointments to count and "list" your home all over
again. I AM NOT INTERESTED IN LISTING YOUR HOME. (IN FACT, I
SOMETIMES TURN DOWN LISTINGS!) 	The only thing that interests me is what
interests you: SELLING your home, for top dollar, fast.
Before you consider signing a new listing contract with any other agent, please
consider the following:
I. I am the Realtor specializing in the Hudson area.
2. 	In the past months, I have sold _ Hudson homes.
3. 	I ONLY "list" properties I am confident I can sell.
4. 	I will be glad to visit with you in person, explain everything I have to offer, and
answer all your questions ... AND I PROMISE: 	no pressure, no “hard sell.”
Your Hudson Community Specialist,
Lucy Listing
PS: Some comments from other clients I've sold houses for are enclosed.

INCREASES FOREVER!

after taxes with as little as a $ 	down payment. We can even help you find the

time home buyers who can qualify with an income as low as $ 	per year. Even
if your income and available cash are limited, alternative financing may be available.

Dear Riverview Homeowner,
Surprising Financial Facts
For Riverview Homeowners:
There Are 3 Very Good
Reasons Why You Might Want
To "Move Up" To Hudson, Now!
My name is Lucy Listing, and I am the Realtor who specializes in the wonderful Hudson
community. 	Recently, I've done a detailed Financial Analysis of YOUR neighborhood too-
and I've discovered 3 very important "financial facts" you ought to know about:
#1: I'M SORRY TO REPORT – YOUR "APPRECIATION" HAS "STALLED"
The houses in your neighborhood are no longer increasing in value. When you bought
your house, you DID make a good investment. 	But now, that investment has peaked in value.
The only way you can keep on using real estate to build your financial security is by moving up
to a new home.
(We'll be happy to give you a copy of the detailed analysis that shows what is happening
to the "stalled" value of your home and why, free, just for the asking. )
#2: RIGHT NOW, WE HAVE BEAUTIFUL HOMES IN HUDSON THAT YOU CAN MOVE
UP TO BUT KEEP VIRTUALLY THE SAME MORTAGE PAYMENT!
That's right – We've determined that YOU have the opportunity to move up to a new
Hudson home ... a home that is INCREASING IN VALUE much faster than the one you're in
right now ... and, considering the equity value you've accumulated in your present home, you'll
be able to keep your monthly mortgage payment about the same. MORE VALUE BUT NOT
MORE MONEY!
Of course, these opportunities ARE LIMITED and MOVING FAST. If this is of any
interest to you at all, you should look into it immediately.
I've enclosed some information about Hudson. But you really need to tour this
wonderful community and some of the luxury homes themselves to fully appreciate how good an
588
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 588 of 625 --

opportunity this is. I'll be glad to give you that tour at your convenience, no obligation, and – I
PROMISE – no pressure.
#3: – NO NEED TO "DISRUPT" YOUR LIFE
Because Hudson is right next to your present neighborhood, you don't have to change
jobs, pull your kids out of school, or, in any way, disrupt your life ... your new, faster-
appreciating home is just down the street!
I URGE YOU TO INVESTIGATE THESE SPECIAL OPPORTUNITIES. 	For the

SALES
Move-Up Letter 3
COPYRIGHT FREE
Photo
Dear Riverview Resident,
Please Don't Take Your Biggest And
Most Important Investment For
Granted.
For most people, the homes they live in are their biggest and most important investments- and
future financial independence depends on that real estate ownership. However, the days when
you could buy just about any home, live in it for 30 years or so, and count on it being a great
investment are long since gone ... and YOUR home is a prime example of this, because THE
APPRECIATION IN YOUR HOME IS VIRTUALLY "STALLED."
Two years ago, every time you made a monthly mortgage payment, you were also
making a terrific investment.
But right now, this month, that's no longer true. And it may not be in the future either.
That's why some smart Riverview owners are using an "equity move" to acquire a beautiful NEW
home in next-door Hudson ... getting more value ... a property that IS increasing steadily in
value ... but still keeping about the same mortgage payment. I know this may sound "harsh" –
but aren't you neglecting your responsibility to your financial security and your family's
financial security by NOT investigating this?
But I know there's also the "emotional side." Maybe you and your family just love your
present home, and I can sure understand that – but maybe you'd fall in love all over again with
one of these full-featured, beautiful new homes in Hudson, too. (Why not take a look-see?)
As the Realtor specializing in Hudson properties, I'd welcome the opportunity of
showing you these beautiful homes and discussing the financial aspects of the opportunity that
exists for you.
Sincerely,
Lucy Listing
591
www.getwsodownload.com

Exhibit Contributors – Listed Alphabetically
Russ Bannister - Russ Bannister's contribution to this manual is a piece that was so successful
and impressive that it took him from managing a Bread Depot to Professional Copywriter
when he showed it to the person interviewing him.
Copywriting For Conversions
www.RussBannister.com
Brian Bergh - Dr. Brian Bergh has served the Glendale, California area as an orthodontic
specialist, providing Invisalign, Invisalign Teen, and braces for children, teens, and adults. For
over 20 years, he’s been providing state-of-the-art orthodontic treatment in a friendly, patient-

-- 606 of 625 --

18 Steps to Consider When Developing Your USP
1. 	What do you do great?
2. 	How it will benefit your target market?
3. 	Clearly identifies your target market
4. 	Opportunity Gap: What void in the marketplace can you fulfill?
5. 	Should be short and easy to relay
6. 	Once someone hears it, will it begin a conversation?
7. 	A great guarantee
8. 	Delivers speed
9. 	Believable
10. 	A big promise
11. 	What do people hate that you can fix?
12. 	Price advantage
13. 	How it’s paid for as an Advantage?
14. 	What do your customers experience?
15. 	Sales process advantage
16. 	Niche
17. 	Personality
18. 	All of the Above

Level 1: Demographic Information Examples
 Age
 Gender
 Income
 Family Size
 Where they live
 Ethnicity
 Level of Education
Level 2: Psychographic Information Examples
 Values
 Interests
 Hobbies
 Political Affiliation
 Religious Views
 Lifestyle
 Attitude
 Personality
Level 3: “Psychic”graphic Information Examples
 What keeps them up at night, staring at the
ceiling? Worry, pain or frustration?
 What is their single biggest problem that you
can solve?
 What do the secretly, privately desire most?
 Fill in the blanks for them:
“If I could just _______ then ___________”
Demographic Info
Psychographic Info
“Psychic”graphic Info
Defining Your Ideal Customer, Client or Patient

-- 622 of 625 --

Are you a soft, sexy, exciting lady
who would like to have a little taste
of part-time paradise?
If so, read on.
My name is Gary and I am
looking for a very special woman
who would like to share a few small
(but exciting!) adventures with me
and who wants to enjoy a part-time
slice of the good life. Are you that
woman? Maybe. Maybe not. The
first thing it depends on is me. You
see, if I'm not your kind of guy, then
what I have to offer may not be your
idea of how life should be lived.
So let's start with me. Here's what
my life is like. First of all, I'm an
early riser. I usually get up around 6
a.m. and eat a piece of fruit and
drink a cup of coffee, and then, on
most days, by 6:30 a.m. I'm jogging
around Lake Hollywood. How far I
run depends on how good I feel. It's
never less than three miles and
seldom more than seven. When I'm
finished, I get into my car and drive
to a place I call "The House of Pain"
(actually 	the 	sign 	outside 	says
"Vince's Gym") where a stone-age
sadist who masquerades as a fitness
instructor 	forces 	me 	to 	use
dumbbells, 	barbells, 	and 	other
fiendish contraptions in ways for
which my body was not designed.
Whatever. After about an hour of
this, 	I 	travel 	to 	yet 	another
establishment 	where 	I give 	my
tired, hurting body a chance to
recover while I rest upon a UVA
suntan bed and listen to soothing
music 	(usually 	Rod 	Stewart 	or
Jimmy Buffet) on a pair of stereo
headphones.
By the time I am finished, it is
approximately 9:45 a.m. and what I
do next is go home, shower, change
into fresh clothes and eat a light
breakfast.
Finally, after all this, I go to work.
And boy, do I ever work! I love
what I do for a living, and, I must
confess, I am truly a workaholic.
For 	example, 	right 	now 	I am
attempting 	to 	put 	together 	the
financial and promotional packages
for 10 different feature films. I am
writing two books (one fiction and
one non-fiction). I am collaborating
on a screenplay and I am attending
to the details of two businesses I
own personally, and also, to the
business details of several corporate
and personal clients whose names
are household words.
It's quite a workload.
And what do I do after I stop
working? What is my big reward for
all this running and grunting and
pumping iron and stretching and
straining and writing and thinking
and solving and creating and caring
and so on?
Nothing, that's what! Nada. Zip.
Not doodley squat. No "Miller
Time!" No drugs. No sex. No rock
and roll.
Not even a little wine and some
quiet classical music.
Why? The answer is simple. You
see, for the last 3-1/2 months, I have
been spending my evenings and
weekends on a marathon of non-
stop sulking.
Why have I been sulking? Good
question. And, 	once 	again, 	the
answer is simple. You see, up until
3-1/2 	months 	ago, 	my 	"Miller
Time" was terrific. It was terrific
because there was a very beautiful,
very erotic, very special lady in my
life and we were in a relationship I
thought would last forever. But, that
relationship has ended. It has ended
stupidly, tragically, and for insane
reasons totally beyond the ability of
any human to control.
Well, such is life. But what's done
is done and 3-1/2 months' worth of
sulking is more than enough for
anyone and now it is time for me to
climb up out of my sulk and find
myself another special woman.
So why write an ad? Why do I
have to advertise for a woman? Am
I some kind of geek with two heads
and bad breath?
No, I am not. I'm a reasonably
attractive 	(maybe 	even 	semi-
handsome?) caucasian male in his
mid-forties 	with 	a 	sparkling
personality 	(except 	when 	I'm
sulking), a keen wit, a steady hand,
and a clear eye. I've got a good tan,
dark brown hair and a short, neatly
trimmed dark brown beard with a
couple of "interesting" spots of
grey. I am of average size. Not
short, not tall; not fat, not skinny.
I'm in excellent health. I'm not
hurting for money and I can look
any maitre d' in the country right
square in the eye without flinching.
So once again, why do I have to
advertise to get a woman? Well,
actually, I don't. I've been married
twice. I've had a few other serious
relationships and, of course, my
share of one-night stands and short-
term romances. I've enjoyed the
company of a few really outstanding
ladies and I want to do so again.
But you know what else? I've also
met many ladies who were not so
outstanding. In fact, I've met more
than a few women who, although
they had great exteriors, they were,
on the inside, flat out bummers!
Want some examples? You do?
OK, you asked for it. Try these out
for size.
Zelda The Princess. Zelda is a
667-2	2620 	C	CLASSIFIED 	6	667-2	2620
Are you more than just another pretty face?
Generous, 	Creative 	Businessman
Wants To Find A Hot, Sexy Woman
With A Good Sense Of Humor
623
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 623 of 625 --

26-year-old 	Jewish 	lady 	who
waltzed 	into 	my 	office 	and
immediately informed me (before I
even had a chance to say hello) she
wanted me to write an ad for her and
she wanted to go to bed with me.
Well, what the hell, on some days
I'm a pushover. She got what she
wanted and I must admit, she gave
me 	one 	of 	the 	most 	thorough
screwings I've ever had in my life.
Unfortunately, it didn't happen in
bed.
And, what happened in bed was
unfortunate also. You see, Zelda's
idea of good sex is brutality. She
wants a man who will slap her
around, degrade and humiliate her
and, quite literally, bounce her off
the walls.
Sorry, but that's not for me. I like
to make love with a woman, not
war.
Sherry The Tragic. Sherry was a
secretary and a go-go dancer. Great
body, a very pretty face and a good
sense of humor. Unfortunately, she
was 	also 	a 	"walking 	accident"
looking for a place to happen. She
was always in court on charges
relating to neglecting her four-year-
old daughter and her ex-boyfriend
was a Mafia hit man (true) who
wanted her back and was trying to
find her.
We had a very brief affair.
Karen 	The 	"Would-Be"
Prostitute. 	A 	gorgeous 	woman
who, after our affair got going,
confessed to me she wanted to live
her life as a hooker. Then she
informed me she wanted me to be
her first "John" and I should start
paying her for sex. When I refused,
she decided I would be her lord and
master (pimp) and she would have
sex with other men and make them
pay and then give the money to me.
This also was a very brief affair.
Claudia 	The 	Actress.
Sensational looks. A real traffic
stopper. I used her in a few full-page
ads 	and 	I 	created 	a 	perfume
promotion based around her. We
started hanging out with each other
and I was the envy of all the men
who saw us together.
Except me. I wasn't envious of me
at all. Claudia had a terminal case of
tunnel vision. The only thing she
could focus on whatsoever for more
than 10-seconds was her precious
career.
She was deadly dull. I couldn't
sustain enough interest in her to
even take her to bed.
I could go on and on. All of these
examples (except for the names) are
true. They have not been made up.
In fact, they have been toned down!
And 	so 	far, 	I 	haven't 	even
described what I consider the worst
category of women at all.
These are women who, in my
opinion, might actually be clinically
crazy. You want to know how I can
tell? It's easy. You see, these are all
the women who do not have any of
the drawbacks that turn me off, and
who, for some inexplicable reason,
are not interested in me.
Can you imagine that?
What do I want in a woman?
Well, I've got a pretty good idea but
I am I must admit, quite flexible.
However, I have a very clear idea of
what I don't want and it is here I am
not flexible at all. So let's start with
that. Here then are...
7 Things Gary Does
NOT Want From A Woman!
#1. DEATH OR DISEASE. This
is my number one no-no. Listen,
I've never had sex with a gay man, a
bi-sexual man, a transsexual man or
any kind of man at all. I hardly ever
go near Santa Monica Boulevard
and, when I do the only place I ever
stop is Barney's Beanery.
And, 	even 	then, 	I never 	eat
quiche.
In other words, I'm straight. Also,
I'm not a hemophiliac. I've never
had a blood transfusion. I'm not a
junkie and I never stick needles into
my body. I'm not promiscuous. I
don't mess around with prostitutes (I
tried it years ago and it was boring),
and I've never even been close to
Africa or Haiti.
What this means, of course, is
with any kind of luck at all (knock
on wood) I do not have AIDS. Also,
to my knowledge, I do not have any
other 	type 	of 	dreadful
communicable disease including
syphilis, 	gonorrhea, 	herpes,
hepatitis, 	or 	even 	the 	bubonic
plague.
If you can't say the same, please,
please do NOT respond to this ad.
#2. DRUG DRAMAS. Do you
like to drink a little or get a little
high once in a while so you can
loosen up and party down? You do?
Good! That means you and I can
have some fun. But please... read
that first sentence again. See where
it says "a little" and "every once in a
while"? Those words are important
to me. Therefore, if your idea of "a
little" and "every once in a while" is
to get drunk or stoned every day, if
the way you like to use cocaine is by
freebasing or injecting it, if you
gulp down Valium or Quaaludes by
the fistful, if you use PCP or heroin
in any way, shape or form, then I
must, once again, ask you to please
NOT answer this ad.
#3. DESPERATE DILEMMAS.
Are 	you 	sleeping 	in 	your 	car
because your rent is six months
overdue? Is 	your 	ex-husband 	a
hatchet murderer who is trying to
track you down and who swears to
mutilate any man who so much as
looks at you? Are you in desperate
need of fast money because your
poor old mother needs a kidney
transplant in order to keep on
living?
I'm sorry. I really am. But I'm just
an ordinary everyday nice guy. I'm
not Superman or even Lee Ioaccoa.
I'm very compassionate and very
understanding but I have recently
retired from trying to save the
world.
Therefore, I'm not qualified to
save your life. However, if you will
let me, I might be able to enhance it
by adding to it some excitement and
romance.
#4. 	MARRIAGE. 	I've 	been
married twice and, both times it
spoiled a great romance. I don't
want to get married again and I don't
want to live with you either. You
see, at this point in my life, I don't
want to own a woman. I just want to
enjoy one.
It would be nice if you decide to
answer this ad, if you already have
some sort of life of your own. I don't
want to be your everything. I would
much 	rather 	be 	that 	special
somebody who you see two or three
times a week and who makes you
feel good.
Would that be OK?
#5. I DON'T WANT A SEXUAL
SWINGER. Do you spend your
evenings attending orgies at the A-
Frame? Do you have a lifetime
membership 	at 	Plato's? Do 	you
refuse to call yourself a prostitute
even though you run a credit check
on every man you meet before you
go to bed with him? Do you have a
time clock in your panties and a
cash register in your bra?
624
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 624 of 625 --

Sorry, we're not compatible.
You know, even though I've been
married and I've been around, I feel
that 	by 	Southern 	California
standards, I'm almost a virgin. For
example, someone told me recently
Hugh Hefner has been to bed with
more than 3,000 women.
Could that be true? I don't know
but, if it is, he is in my opinion, a
man to be pitied, not to be envied.
I'll take quality over quantity any
day.
#6. I DON'T WANT A SEXUAL
PRUDE. I bet by now you think I'm
repressed, don't you? I bet you think
the hot throb of lust does not live in
my loins. I bet you think if you and
Kelly LeBrock showed up at my
door 	with 	a 	suitcase 	full 	of
excitement from Trashy Lingerie
(they're located at 402 N. La Cienga
and they've got the hottest stuff in
town!) and suggested we have a
menage a trois I would toss you
both out on your ear and report you
to Jerry Falwell.
You are wrong. You are wrong.
You are wrong. You are wrong.
Fear not. I may be cautious but
I'm not crazy.
Hark 	unto 	me. 	Listen. 	Just
because I'm not into freebasing,
orgies and non-stop promiscuity
doesn't mean I'm dead. It's true I
don't want a woman who's been
sleeping with everything in pants.
However on the other hand, if you
are a 35-year-old virgin who thinks
foreplay should be 1/2 hour of
begging and "oral sex" is the name
of a disgusting new group of punk
rockers then, you may rest assured,
our stars were simply not meant to
cross.
#7. I DON'T WANT A WOMAN
WHO 	CAN'T 	STAND
PROSPERITY. Don't laugh. I lost
the love of my life because things
got "too good." Some people are
into the struggle and not the reward.
I'm into both. As you already
know, I like to work but work
without 	reward 	is 	senseless. 	It
seems to me many women (and
men) just insist on filling up their
lives with a lot of needless trauma.
Not me. I want the payoff along
with the pain. Therefore...
"If you don't want the good
And just want the bad,
Don't waste your time
By answering this ad!"
Good Lord, that was corny, wasn't
it?
Well, anyway, that's my laundry
list of what I don't want and in fact,
what I can't handle. Now comes the
hard part. I really feel awkward
about saying what I do want. I'm
afraid if I get too explicit it will
seem like I'm an insensitive clod
ordering something from a Chinese
menu.
On the other hand, if I don't set
down some guidelines, I'm afraid
this ad will be answered by many
women with whom I would not be
at all compatible.
So please, give me a break. I'm
not nearly as definite about what I
am about to write as it will appear in
print. Remember, what I am about
to write is not etched in stone.
Anyway, here I go. My idea of a
perfect woman is someone who is
intelligent and healthy with a good
sense of humor and someone who
will take my breath away when I see
her in a string bikini!
As far as age is concerned, if you
are somewhere between 25 and 35
that would be just fine and, if you
are a little younger or a little older,
that is probably no big deal.
I like women who take care of
themselves. If you have a slender,
healthy body, a reasonably slim
waist, rather generous breasts (God
that sounds redneck, doesn't it?), a
very pretty face and a good sense of
humor then quite frankly, you sound
like heaven to me!
So much for specifications.
And now if after all this, you are
still interested, what can you expect
from me? Well, the first word in the
headline of this ad is "Generous"
and 	I 	am 	just 	that. 	However,
generous does not mean "chump." It
also doesn't mean I want to pay for
sex. That's ridiculous. Any man in
L.A. who wants to pay for sex
doesn't have to write an ad, all he
has to do is answer one. Those ads
are all over, even in the yellow
pages.
Here's what I mean by generous. I
love to buy presents for women. I
like to take them to movies and
plays and I love to send flowers and
buy them jewelry and clothes and, if
I really get involved with a woman,
I rather enjoy helping to support her
and helping her to elevate her
lifestyle.
Also, I give great vacations. I love
to travel for long weekends (four
days or so) to Acapulco, Hawaii,
Fort Lauderdale, the Bahamas, and
so on. I only fly first class and I try
to always stay in the best hotels and
eat in the best restaurants.
Does any of this sound good to
you? I hope so. This is an honest ad.
Every word is true and although I've
made a modest attempt to make it
entertaining, you should also know I
am sincere.
Are you leery about answering a
personal ad? I don't blame you. I
sure am. Before I decided to write
this 	ad 	I 	started 	reading 	other
"personal ads" and they scare the
hell out of me. I'm always afraid
they are being written by sexually
sick 	people 	or 	real 	losers 	and
sometimes 	by 	people 	who 	are
downright dangerous.
I mean have you read those ads?
They go like this:
"Psychotic white woman wants
to be sodomized by 12 cuban
truck 	drivers 	and 	a 	boa
constrictor 	while 	husband
watches and salivates. Call 1-
800-SICKY"
Or like this:
"96-year-old lady with youthful
outlook 	wants 	to 	meet
vegetarian 	non-smoker 	to
discuss saving the whales and
other ecological concerns."
Or:
"Pleasingly plump 590 pound
woman wants to meet sincere
fun loving man to care for her
and her lovely 18 children."
And so on.
I'm not like that. Really, I'm not. I
promise. I'm a reasonably normal
healthy male who would like to add
a little excitement and romance to
his life with a reasonably normal,
healthy female.
If you are at all interested or even
curious, please write and tell me
about yourself and how to get in
touch with you and, also, please
send a recent full length photo.
Who knows. Maybe we'll click
and maybe we won't. But, at the
very least, you won't be writing to
some sick psychotic and maybe, just
maybe, it will all turn out great.
Just write to:
Gary, Box 208, 8033 Sunset Blvd.
Los Angeles, CA 90046
Thank you for your support.
625
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 625 of 625 --
